results.question: How many customers are there?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers

results.question: What is our total revenue?
results.gen_sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

results.question: What is the average order value?
results.gen_sql: SELECT AVG(total_amount) AS average_order_value FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

results.question: How many products are in the catalog?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products

results.question: What is the highest order amount?
results.gen_sql: SELECT MAX(total_amount) FROM orders

results.question: How many orders have been placed in total?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders

results.question: How many platinum-tier customers do we have?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers WHERE tier = 'platinum'

results.question: How many orders are in completed status?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

results.question: How many products cost more than 400 dollars?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products WHERE price > 400

results.question: How many refunded orders are there?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'refunded'

results.question: How many products are in the electronics category?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products WHERE category = 'electronics'

results.question: How many gold or silver customers are there?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers WHERE tier IN ('gold', 'silver')

results.question: What is the total revenue from gold tier customers?
results.gen_sql: SELECT SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND c.tier = 'gold'

results.question: How many orders has each customer tier placed?
results.gen_sql: SELECT c.tier, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.tier

results.question: Top 5 customers by total completed revenue
results.gen_sql: SELECT c.id, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5

results.question: Total revenue by customer tier
results.gen_sql: SELECT c.tier, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.tier ORDER BY revenue DESC

results.question: Which customer has placed the most orders?
results.gen_sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 1

results.question: How many customers have never placed an order?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers c WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.id)

results.question: What is the revenue by product category, for completed orders only?
results.gen_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY revenue DESC

results.question: What are the top 3 best-selling products by total quantity?
results.gen_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id, p.name ORDER BY total_quantity DESC LIMIT 3

results.question: Which product category has generated the most revenue?
results.gen_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 1

results.question: How many units have been sold in each product category, for completed orders?
results.gen_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity) AS units_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY units_sold DESC

results.question: What is the total revenue by customer tier and product category, for completed orders?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT c.tier, p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue 
FROM order_items oi 
JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id 
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id 
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id 
WHERE o.status = 'completed' 
GROUP BY c.tier, p.category 
ORDER BY revenue DESC

results.question: How many distinct products has each customer purchased?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT c.id AS customer_id, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.product_id) AS distinct_products_purchased 
FROM customers c 
JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id 
JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id 
GROUP BY c.id

results.question: Total revenue by customer tier | just gold and platinum
results.gen_sql: SELECT SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND c.tier IN ('gold', 'platinum')
results.explanation: [session=bench-fdeb4b05 turn=2] rewritten="What is the total revenue for customers in the gold and platinum tiers?"

results.question: How many products in each category? | only those priced over 100
results.gen_sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products p WHERE p.price > 100 GROUP BY p.category
results.explanation: [session=bench-7c4c25ae turn=2] rewritten="How many products in each category are priced over 100?"

results.question: Top 10 customers by revenue | sort by descending revenue and show only gold tier
results.gen_sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND c.tier = 'gold' GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10
results.explanation: [session=bench-21364f86 turn=2] rewritten="What are the top 10 gold tier customers sorted by descending revenue?"

results.question: Running total of completed revenue by month
results.gen_sql:
SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', created_at) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS completed_revenue
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'completed'
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month

results.question: What share of total completed revenue comes from gold-tier customers?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT SUM(o.total_amount) * 1.0 / (SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed') AS gold_tier_revenue_share 
FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id 
WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND c.tier = 'gold'

results.question: What is the average revenue per completed order?
results.gen_sql: SELECT AVG(total_amount) AS average_revenue_per_order FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

results.question: How much revenue comes from customers whose total spend is above the overall average customer spend?
results.gen_sql: SELECT SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND c.id IN (SELECT c2.id FROM customers c2 JOIN orders o2 ON c2.id = o2.customer_id WHERE o2.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c2.id HAVING SUM(o2.total_amount) > (SELECT AVG(total_spend) FROM (SELECT SUM(o3.total_amount) AS total_spend FROM orders o3 WHERE o3.status = 'completed' GROUP BY o3.customer_id)))

results.question: Monthly completed revenue with the change vs the previous month
results.gen_sql:
SELECT 
    strftime('%Y-%m', o.created_at) AS month,
    SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue,
    SUM(o.total_amount) - COALESCE(LAG(SUM(o.total_amount)) OVER (ORDER BY strftime('%Y-%m', o.created_at)), 0) AS change
FROM 
    orders o 
WHERE 
    o.status = 'completed' 
GROUP BY 
    month 
ORDER BY 
    month

results.question: How many orders per day of week?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT strftime('%w', orders.created_at) AS day_of_week, COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM orders
GROUP BY day_of_week

results.question: What was the total completed revenue in Q4 2025?
results.gen_sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' AND created_at >= '2025-10-01' AND created_at < '2026-01-01'

results.question: What was the first order date for each customer?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT customers.id AS customer_id, MIN(orders.created_at) AS first_order_date
FROM customers
INNER JOIN orders ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
GROUP BY customers.id

results.question: Top 2 customers in each tier by total completed revenue
results.gen_sql:
SELECT c.tier, c.id, c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY c.tier, c.id, c.name
ORDER BY c.tier, total_revenue DESC LIMIT 2

results.question: Which customers have total spend above the average total spend across all customers?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend 
FROM customers c 
JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id 
WHERE o.status = 'completed' 
GROUP BY c.id, c.name 
HAVING total_spend > (SELECT AVG(orders.total_amount) FROM orders WHERE orders.status = 'completed')

results.question: What is the least-sold product in the electronics category, by total units sold?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT p.id, p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_units_sold 
FROM order_items oi 
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id 
WHERE p.category = 'electronics' 
GROUP BY p.id, p.name 
ORDER BY total_units_sold ASC 
LIMIT 1

results.question: How many customers placed at least 5 completed orders?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS customer_count FROM orders o WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY o.customer_id HAVING COUNT(o.id) >= 5

results.question: List all products that have never been ordered
results.gen_sql:
SELECT p.id, p.name, p.category, p.price 
FROM products p 
LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id 
WHERE oi.product_id IS NULL

results.question: Customers whose average completed order value exceeds 1500
results.gen_sql: SELECT c.id, c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name HAVING AVG(o.total_amount) > 1500

results.question: How many product categories have at least one product priced above 400?
results.gen_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) FROM products p WHERE p.price > 400

results.question: Which customer placed the single most expensive completed order?
results.gen_sql:
SELECT c.id, c.name, MAX(o.total_amount) AS max_order_amount 
FROM customers c 
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id 
WHERE o.status = 'completed' 
GROUP BY c.id, c.name 
ORDER BY max_order_amount DESC 
LIMIT 1

results.question: Revenue by month for completed orders | just March and April 2026
results.gen_sql:
SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', o.created_at) AS month, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue 
FROM orders o 
WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.created_at >= '2026-03-01' AND o.created_at < '2026-05-01' 
GROUP BY month 
ORDER BY month
results.explanation: [session=bench-35412be1 turn=2] rewritten="What was the revenue by month for completed orders specifically in March and April 2026?"

results.question: Top 10 customers by total completed revenue | only gold tier
results.gen_sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND c.tier = 'gold' GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10
results.explanation: [session=bench-79f073d5 turn=2] rewritten="What are the top 10 gold tier customers by total completed revenue?"

results.question: List products in the electronics category | sort by price descending | just the top 5
results.gen_sql: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.price FROM products p WHERE p.category = 'electronics' ORDER BY p.price DESC LIMIT 5
results.explanation: [session=bench-161c3376 turn=3] rewritten="Can you list the top 5 products in the electronics category and sort them by price in descending order?"

results.question: Total completed revenue by customer tier | as a percentage of the grand total
results.explanation: [session=bench-74066ac9 turn=2] Cannot answer: The question does not specify what metric or value should be expressed as a percentage of the grand total.
